title: Found and removed old repository definitions of model DICT, as they are out-dated
breadcrumbs: Found and removed old repository definitions of model DICT, as they are out-dated
text: Found and removed old repository definitions of model DICT, as they are out-dated This message is generated after data import, when the Migration Utility identifies Uniface 9 DICT definitions, and deletes them. Problem Uniface 10 cannot accept Uniface 9 meta-dictionary definitions for the DICT model. Resolution None. Sample Message === MIGRATION INFO [version 9 repository definitions found] === Found and removed old repository definitions of model DICT, as they are out-dated. 6813 - Total number of records/rows imported: 2997. Copy Note: The 6813 message reports 2997 records were imported, but this is misleading. They were imported during the import process, but deleted during the migration process which was started by the import process. The import process only reports the number of records physically imported, not the number that are actually left after the migration process completes. Related Links Meta Dictionary The meta dictionary consists of modeled entities that describe the Uniface Repository itself. It is delivered as a file called umeta.xml in the uniface/misc directory of your installation. This file also includes some other database tables used by Uniface at runtime. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
